findings.title: R1 length matches the expected fixed structure

evidence.note: R1 should be exactly 28 bp (sum of its fixed-length segments)

findings.title: Cell barcodes on the 10x whitelist

evidence.note: R1[0:16] should match the 3M-february-2018 gel-bead barcode whitelist

findings.detail: 94.9% of cell barcodes are on the whitelist

findings.title: R2 reads beginning with the TSO (adapter-dimer / short insert)

evidence.note: R2 should start with cDNA; a leading TSO is the hallmark of empty/short-insert products

findings.detail: 3.8% of R2 start with the TSO

findings.title: R1 read-through into the 3' adapter

evidence.note: the AGATCGGAAGAGCACA adapter stem in R1 means the insert was shorter than the read length

findings.detail: 0.0% of R1 contain the adapter stem AGATCGGAAGAGCACA

findings.title: R2 read-through into the 3' adapter

evidence.note: the AGATCGGAAGAGCACA adapter stem in R2 means the insert was shorter than the read length

findings.detail: 0.1% of R2 contain the adapter stem AGATCGGAAGAGCACA

findings.title: R2 with a poly-G no-signal tail

evidence.note: a poly-G 3' tail on two-color instruments is 'no signal' — typical of empty/short fragments

findings.detail: 0.2% of R2 have a poly-G tail

ranked.why: 3.8% of R2 reads begin with the TSO — the highest-signal finding, but still below the < 0.05 threshold (severity 0.077). It indicates a minor level of empty/short-insert (adapter-dimer) products, not a failure.
0.2% of R2 carry a poly-G no-signal tail, far under the < 0.01 threshold; negligible empty/short-fragment two-color artifact.
0.1% of R2 contain the AGATCGGAAGAGCACA adapter stem, well under < 0.02; insert sizes are appropriate for the 91 bp R2.
94.9% of cell barcodes match the 3M-february-2018 whitelist, above the >= 0.85 gate — barcode read and gel-bead chemistry are correct.
R1 is exactly 28 bp across all reads, matching the 16bp CB + 12bp UMI v3/v3.1 fixed structure.
0.0% adapter stem in R1; the 28 bp barcode read never reaches the 3' adapter, exactly as expected.

plan.root_cause: No library-prep or sequencing failure detected — the library is nominal. Every check passes; the only nonzero-but-sub-threshold signal is a modest TSO-led-R2 fraction, attributable to a small background of empty/short-insert (adapter-dimer) products inherent to 10x 3' GEX prep.
plan.diagnosis: This 10x Chromium 3' v3/v3.1 library looks healthy: R1 is a clean 28 bp (16 bp barcode + 12 bp UMI), 94.9% of cell barcodes are on the gel-bead whitelist, and adapter read-through and poly-G tails are all well under threshold. The only mild flag is that 3.8% of R2 reads start with the TSO — a small background of empty/short-insert products — but it stays below the 5% action limit. No intervention needed; proceed to alignment and standard cell-calling QC.
